theory.intro: Two simple summaries describe a data display: the **range** (how spread out the values are) and the **mode** (the value that appears most often).
facts: **Mode** is the value with the most dots, or the tallest column.
A data set can have **no mode** (all equal) or more than one mode.
steps: Find the smallest and largest values and subtract to get the range.
Find the value that occurs most often — that is the mode.
To compare two displays, work out each one's range and mode.

foundation.q: Find the range of this dot plot.
Find the mode of the same dot plot.
Find the range of this dot plot.
Find the mode of the same dot plot.
Find the range of this column graph.
Find the mode of this dot plot.
Find the range of this dot plot.
Find the mode of this column graph.
Find the range of this dot plot.
Find the mode of the same dot plot.

development.q: For this dot plot, find both the range and the mode.
Two dot plots are shown. Which has the larger range, $X$ or $Y$?
Using the two dot plots above, which has mode $3$?
A column graph shows shoe sizes $5, 6, 7, 8$ with counts $2, 5, 3, 1$. What is the mode size?
Using the shoe size graph, what is the range of the sizes?
Class A's test scores have range $20$ and mode $70$. Class B's have range $40$ and mode $70$. Which class's scores are more spread out?
A dot plot of goals per game is shown. Find the range.
Using the goals dot plot, find the mode.
A dot plot shows values $3, 3, 4, 4$ (two dots each). What is the mode?
A dot plot has all four values different, each one dot. What is the mode?

mastery.q: Two dot plots show daily temperatures for two weeks. Week $1$ has range $4$, mode $20$; week $2$ has range $8$, mode $22$. Which week was more changeable, and which was warmer most often?
A dot plot has range $6$ with smallest value $2$. What is the largest value?
Data: $4, 7, 7, 9, 11$. Find the range and mode.
Adding one more dot at value $9$ to a plot of $1, 3, 9$ changes the mode. What is the new mode?
Class A scores: range $30$, mode $80$. Class B scores: range $30$, mode $60$. They have the same spread — what differs?
A column graph of pets has counts $3, 3, 3$ for cat, dog, fish. What is the mode?
Two displays of scores have the same mode but A has range $10$ and B has range $50$. Which set has a more consistent group of scores?
A dot plot of family sizes has dots at $2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 6$. Find the range and mode, then say which is bigger.
A set of $5$ values has range $0$. What does this tell you about the values?
Two surveys of pocket money both have mode $\$10$. Survey A range is $\$5$, survey B range is $\$30$. Explain what the larger range in B means.
mastery.a: B's amounts are more spread out / more varied
